steps.title: Identify what the sentence must show
steps.content: The goal is to emphasize how few of the park's islands have Komodo dragons. A strong emphasis requires both numbers (4 dragons-islands out of 29 total) and a word that signals scarcity.

steps.title: Test each choice against the requirement
steps.content:
• "Komodo dragons, the world's largest lizards, live on islands in Komodo National Park, Indonesia.": Confirms that dragons live on islands, but gives no specific numbers and no sense of how rare that is. ✗
  • "The largest lizards in the world are found in Komodo National Park.": Identifies the lizards' size record but omits the island count entirely. ✗
  • "Only four of the twenty-nine islands in Komodo National Park have Komodo dragons living on them.": Uses "only" to signal scarcity and supplies both numbers, making the proportion concrete and emphatic. ✓
  • "There are twenty-nine islands in Indonesia's Komodo National Park.": Gives the total island count but drops the dragon count, so no comparison or emphasis is possible. ✗
That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
